model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Michigan State is the vastly superior team as a 3-seed with a 25-7 record, while North Dakota State is a 14-seed from the Summit League with a significantly weaker schedule (SOS -5.87). The Spartans' elite defensive efficiency and tournament experience under Tom Izzo should overwhelm the Bison, who have not faced a top-115 KenPom team all season. ESPN Analytics gives Michigan State a 94.2% win probability, and the 16.5-point spread reflects the massive talent disparity, making a 14-over-3 upset extremely unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan State vs North Dakota State Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan State is a heavy favorite with superior metrics, experience, and schedule strength against a North Dakota State team that lacks quality wins.
reasoningStep.evidence: Michigan State is a 3-seed with a 25-7 record, while North Dakota State is a 14-seed with a 27-7 record but a weak schedule (SOS -5.87, 282nd nationally). ESPN Analytics gives Michigan State a 94.2% win probability, and the Spartans are favored by 16.5 points. North Dakota State's offensive and defensive ratings (ORtg 116.1, DRtg 100.1) are solid but achieved against weak competition, while Michigan State's tournament pedigree under Tom Izzo provides a significant edge in preparation and execution.
